subject: Social and Political Life - II
overall_learning_objectives: Understand that health is more than just being free of illness
Differentiate between Public and Private health services in simple terms
Identify the role of the State Government in providing healthcare
Understand the problems faced by poor people in getting medical treatment
Learn about the 'Costa Rican' approach to health as a global example
Recognize the importance of clean water and sanitation for health
Identify the basic functions of a Public Health Centre (PHC)

lesson_plan.title: What is Health?
topics_covered: Definition of health: Physical and Mental well-being
Factors affecting health (Clean water, pollution-free environment, food)
Comparison: Health facilities in India (The good and the bad)
teaching_strategy: Brainstorming: 'What makes us healthy?'
Discussion on the paradox of India (many doctors but poor facilities in villages)
Visual aids showing clean vs. dirty surroundings
student_activities: Make a list of 5 things needed to stay healthy besides medicine
Discuss: 'Why do we fall ill even when we have doctors?'
assessment: Oral quiz: Is health only about diseases?
Identify two factors that prevent us from being healthy

lesson_plan.title: The Story of Hakim Sheikh
topics_covered: Case study of Hakim Sheikh and his accident
Difficulty in getting admitted to government hospitals
The Supreme Court's ruling on the 'Right to Life'
teaching_strategy: Storytelling based on the NCERT case study
Role-play: Hakim Sheikh at the hospital gate
Explaining that the government must protect our lives
student_activities: Write a short 'Letter of Complaint' from Hakim Sheikh to the Health Minister
Discuss: 'Why was it wrong to turn Hakim Sheikh away?'
assessment: Short answer: Why did Hakim Sheikh go to court?
Defining 'Right to Life' in simple words

lesson_plan.title: Public Health Services
topics_covered: What are Public Health Services?
Public Health Centres (PHC) and District Hospitals
Why are they called 'Public'?
teaching_strategy: Flowchart of public health (Village $ightarrow$ District $ightarrow$ City)
Discussion on why government hospitals are cheap or free
Explaining how the government gets money (Taxes)
student_activities: Draw a simple map showing a village PHC and a city hospital
List 3 reasons why these services are called 'Public'
assessment: Matching: PHC vs. District Hospital jobs
Oral quiz: Where does the government get money for hospitals?

lesson_plan.title: Private Health Services
topics_covered: What are Private Health Services?
Doctors in private clinics and nursing homes
Cost difference between Public and Private
teaching_strategy: Comparing the stories of Aman (Public) and Ranjan (Private)
Discussion on the 'Cost of Treatment'
Using the textbook comic strip to show the two experiences
student_activities: Table: Compare Public vs. Private hospitals (Cost, Cleanliness, Waiting time)
Draw a scene from a private hospital vs. a public hospital
assessment: Identifying which service is more expensive
Naming one benefit of a private hospital

lesson_plan.title: Is Healthcare Available to All?
topics_covered: The rising cost of private medicine
Hardships of the poor (Selling belongings for treatment)
Inequality in health: Women and tribal areas
teaching_strategy: Discussion on why poor people avoid going to doctors
Explaining that 40% of people borrow money for medical bills
Talking about medical stores and high-cost medicines
student_activities: Brainstorm: 'How can we help poor people get medicine?'
assessment: Exit slip: Name one group of people who find it hard to get healthcare

lesson_plan.title: What Can Be Done? (Kerala & Costa Rica)
topics_covered: The Kerala Experience (1996): Giving power to Panchayats
Costa Rica: Choosing health over an army
Importance of safe drinking water
teaching_strategy: Story of the 'Healthiest Country' (Costa Rica)
Discussion on how Kerala improved its villages
Explaining 'Prevention is better than cure'
student_activities: Locate Kerala on an India map and Costa Rica on a world map
Write 2 lines on why Costa Rica has no army
assessment: Oral quiz: Which state gave 40% of its budget to Panchayats?
Identify the key to Costa Rica's health

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Summary
topics_covered: Recap of Public vs. Private
Recap of Hakim Sheikh and Costa Rica
Final vocabulary check: Taxes, PHC, Communicable diseases
teaching_strategy: Fun game: 'Public or Private?' scenario cards
Reviewing NCERT back-exercises
Final doubt-clearing session
student_activities: Complete the crossword puzzle in the notebook
Participate in the 'Health Role' class quiz

teaching_aids: Social and Political Life - II Textbook
Pictures of PHCs and Private Hospitals
Chart on Taxes and Government services
teacher_tip: At this easy level, use the story of Aman and Ranjan extensively. It is the best way for students to relate to the difference between public and private sectors without getting bogged down in difficult terminology.
